reviews.review_msg: The course material is straightforward and lectures are easy to follow along to, but she doesn't set clear grading expectations. for example, she told us our quizzes and midterm would be multiple choice, but instead had filling-in-the-blanks and essay questions. She is also very disorganized and can sometimes be condescending.

Zahra is very disorganized. She is passionate about what she's teaching, but it seems like she doesn't really care about the class. She is not very approachable and can be condescending. I would not recommend taking a class with her.

447* I've honestly never written a review on RMP. Most people knew it was redundant and repetitive of the business classes at Beedie. I was baffled when we were still talking about what qualitative observations and quantitative observations was in the 3rd week. Keeps reiterating how much time it took to create the course. (uses 1996 & 2016 graph)
I second what many have said about her being defensive, unorganized and unprepared, but the weirdest thing for me was that she refers to herself as "Dr. Zahra".
Disorganized, makes phone calls in class, talks about her private matters, uses outdated materials, gives unclear instructions, and is extremely defensive. I didn't like her.
She is absolutely an amazing prof! One of the best profs I had. Truly cares about her students and makes lectures fun with in class activities that help us learn the course concepts for that week
The other reviews are just too harsh zahra really is a good prof and truly cares about her students!

Through her style of teaching it is evident that she knows what she is doing and truly cares about the well being of students. She put a lot of time and effort into explaining course material in detail which was greatly appreciated. When asked questions she happily answered them and grading is fair. Overall, great prof and definitely recommend.
Very neurotic and defensive. Very vague with assignment expectations and deducts lots of points for minor mistakes. Worst teacher ever.

She's very unorganized and vague with her assignments. Gets mad at students for asking questions when she gives confusing instructions. I would avoid her if possible, learned absolutely nothing from this course.

Like other reviews have mentioned, Zahra is very unorganized. Her assignment instructions are very vague/not written down but she gets mad when students ask her questions to clarify. The curve is very high and the reflection is pretty much pointless. I'd avoid taking her classes if you can.

Worst marketing prof I have ever. She will randomly mute her zoom to talk to her kids mid sentence. Has not updated syllabus in years and gets annoyed when people ask questions about it. She also says I already said that and does not host office hours. No idea why she became a prof if she doesnt want to deal with questions. Groups are random gl

I think some of the past reviews were just too harsh! Zahra is somewhat disorganized, but her lectures were informative and she cares about her students. She's an easy marker, so you will have to perfect most of the assignments to beat the curve and get an A. She can be sassy at times, but she's an overall decent prof and a sweet person!

She doesn't have a clear grading criteria for anything and there are no proper instructions. Even if you talk to her, youre still left guessing what she wants. She's an easy marker to the curve is really high so beware! I would not recommend. What a waste of my time.

Worst SFU business prof. Majority of lecture time is spent on technical difficulties, being muted for personal matters, ranting to the class about personal matters, or making jokes/puns with her favorite students. A waste of time and money. Little instructions are given for projects. Student questions are not properly answered. Avoid if you can.

Zahra is a great prof! She wants her students to do well and is a fairly easy marker, however I find that may not be the best for Beedie considering we are graded on a curve :(. Everyone basically gets the same marks for everything other than the midterm so be sure to put your all into that. Also, make sure to reach out to her outside class often.

The only mark that matters is the exam as everyone receive the same/similar marks for the other things. You need to do well in the exam to do well in this class. The exam is not related to the textbook, so i suggest only focusing on topics are red in the slides. Zahra is a easy marker & never teaches the full lecture. Overall I recommend her class
From what I have seen in class, her teaching style and personality are better reflected by the reviews she received when she was at UBC rather than SFU

The midterm was not very relevant to the course or study material. It was based on a case study and the questions were more about the article than it was about the application of knowledge. The questions were asking specific details within the article. For example, who were the owners? Did not feel like an exam worth 30%

The lectures go really off topic and don't relate to the textbook very well. Test questions topics are sometimes unrelated to the class. Occasionally snaps at students if they say something she doesn't like or finds boring. Seems very strict on students but not on herself. Any problem she doesn't want to resolve she will say "you're 4th years"
Zahra was a brilliant professor! There is one group project, quizzes, midterms, and attendance! She is a more than fair grader on both the midterm and the group project! Highly recommend!
She's incredible and caring. She's also a major badass- I think everyone wanted to be her. She is a fairly easy marker, there are a bunch of quizzes but honestly it's all good. She's great.

She ended up giving us 10 days to do the last assignment instead of 7 and I found that very helpful! I think she and TAs really want students to do well but the rubrics (that she did not create) are strict. Easy marks from weekly In the News and don't fall behind with readings. Actually go to class.

Zahra is very down to earth and will answer any specific questions you have during the lecture. But it is important to stay focused, take notes, and review after class. Make sure you actually read the survival guide. Her TA Adam was very helpful and often extended his office hour to help students. If you can write decently you will be okay.

Far-reaching and unrelated topics made it difficult to study or do assignments. Zahra fails at relating topics to each other even within the same slide. Reads off a pre-made slide deck while providing no additional information. Declared over half the chapters boring and unpractical. That isn't an excuse. You are the professor. Do something about it

One of SFU Business' top marketing profs!  Extremely passionate about the material, and about teaching in general. She was able to teach difficult concepts in a way that made sense for the entire class!  Definitely hot as well!
She is one of the best bus profs i have ever had. she makes the learning so much interesting. her class is worth going. exams and grading is fair. strongly recommend you take this class with her.
Zahra is bomb! She is a fun professor who actually teaches you something. She is very fair, knows her stuff, and very helpful. Worth going to class she's a cool prof

This was actually the first business class I looked forward to during my entire time at SFU, very easy to participate in her class as she was able to make the classroom atmosphere welcoming to anyone that had input on certain topics. Also very knowledgable on all topics, no confusion at all during classes.

Awesome prof.  You could tell she was really interested in marketing and her interest rubbed off on everyone in the class.  Had interesting discussions in the class and she encourages questions if you dont understand.  Really approachable for extra help.  Exams and assignments are very fair.Excellent professor and I recommend taking a class wit her

Loved this professor! She's brilliant and she has so much real work experience that she shares with the class.  She really cares about her students and makes learning the material interesting and fun! She takes concepts from the book and helps us apply it to ourselves which facilitates learning. Definately Definately Recommeneded!!

Awesome prof. Very interactive class, lots of fun and somehow you acutally learn too! Projects were lots of work, but when you look back at it, they were pretty fun. Wish there were more profs like her on campus. Definitely recommend to take one Bus class with her.

Amazing Prof! She's Brilliant and I have never seen a prof know their stuff better than her- she just glances and the notes and then goes on and on about each topic just from memory! She makes the material interesting and really engages the students into the lecture! Loved her!!

Amazing marketing prof - she clearly knows her stuff, and is able to articulate the information at a level suitable for undergrads!  Absolutely fair in markeing; I totally recommend taking at least one university course with her!
